subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, Banque de France, Freedom House, RSF, ReliefWeb/OCHA) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.

A coup-prone Indian Ocean archipelago (~20 coups/attempts since 1975) with deep Anjouan/Moheli secessionist history and the unresolved Mayotte dispute; President Azali (a former coup leader) won a disputed Jan 2024 election amid post-election unrest.

Standout drags: chronic political fragility, very weak human capital and healthcare, and acute cyclone/volcanic exposure (Cyclone Chido, Dec 2024); the euro-pegged franc and diaspora remittances are the external anchors.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored but weak-revenue, remittance-financed economy with a tiny export base.

fiscal_state.notes: Weak revenue mobilisation (tax revenue down ~12% by mid-2025) and uneven consolidation, though under an IMF ECF. (IMF ECF 4th Review 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The franc's euro peg with an unlimited French convertibility guarantee imports euro-area discipline - limited autonomy but a real anchor. (Banque de France; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The Comorian franc is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Elevated but moderating - ~7.3% y/y in March 2025 on Cyclone Chido supply shocks, anchored over time by the euro peg. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: A small, shallow sector with weak credit, gradual recovery but limited supervision/depth. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A tiny, undiversified export base (vanilla, cloves, ylang-ylang) with a structural trade deficit financed by diaspora remittances. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Weak, politicized institutions with entrenched corruption and a fragile press.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak and politicized - power consolidated post-2018, with a contested judiciary and entrenched corruption. (Freedom House 2025; Heritage)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF program-engaged with usable but limited-capacity statistics. (IMF ECF 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Chronic underinvestment - among the lowest on the Human Capital Index, with weak service delivery. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Mixed/fragile - pressure on the opposition, but 2025 saw first-ever press cards and Parliament rejecting a source-disclosure clause (RSF ~72/180). (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Coup-prone with a disputed election and deadly unrest, over persistent inter-island grievances.

political_stability.notes: A coup-prone history and the disputed Jan 2024 election with deadly unrest - stability rests on consolidated executive power, not durable institutions. (France 24; ISS)

civil_liberties.notes: Restricted - crackdowns on opposition and protest, with reported violence in the 2024 post-election period. (Freedom House 2025; France 24)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A limited foreign population; the dominant migration story is Comorians risking the crossing to Mayotte. (France 24; inference)

social_cohesion.notes: Persistent inter-island grievances (Anjouan/Moheli vs Moroni) and the Mayotte question fuel latent fragmentation. (Britannica; ISS)

demographics.notes: Young but very poor (~45% below the poverty line), with high emigration and weak human capital. (France 24; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: France-dependent and broadly non-aligned, conflict-free but with the Mayotte dispute unresolved.

alliance_reliability.notes: Close, dependency-driven ties to France (currency guarantee, diaspora, aid) plus AU engagement; small and non-aligned. (Banque de France)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active armed conflict, but a history of secessionist insurgencies/interventions and the unresolved Mayotte sovereignty dispute. (AU records)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions; the euro peg's convertibility guarantee supports external stability despite a thin economy. (Banque de France; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Acute cyclone and volcanic exposure, with a severely under-resourced health system.

climate.notes: High exposure to cyclones (Chido, Dec 2024 affected ~64,000) and flooding, with climate-driven supply shocks. (IMF; ReliefWeb/OCHA)

healthcare.notes: Severely under-resourced - shortages of medicines, equipment, and staff, with less than half of pregnant women getting full prenatal care. (World Bank; AHO)

infrastructure.notes: Poor inter-island connectivity, unreliable power/water, and weak transport on a fragmented archipelago. (World Bank; IMF)

crime_safety.notes: Ordinary crime moderate; principal risks are political violence and disaster, not baseline crime. (inference)

disaster_insurance.notes: Acute, compounding hazard (cyclone + Mt Karthala volcanism) with negligible penetration. (Global Volcanism Program; inference)

summary: One of the world's poorest states - a coup-prone Indian Ocean archipelago with deep inter-island secessionist fractures (Anjouan/Moheli) and the unresolved Mayotte dispute. President Azali Assoumani, a former coup leader who removed term limits, won a disputed January 2024 election (62.97% on ~16% turnout) marred by fraud claims and deadly unrest, atop weak institutions and pervasive corruption. Its clearest structural strengths are external anchors rather than domestic capacity: the euro-pegged franc with a French convertibility guarantee, robust diaspora remittances, and an IMF ECF program. The skew is negative - chronic political fragility, very weak human capital and healthcare, and acute cyclone/volcanic exposure (Cyclone Chido hit in Dec 2024) outweigh the monetary anchor.
